Farn Por-rrrcal PRACTTcEs C orraurssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
November 27,200I
Eric Grant
Sweeney &Grant,LLP
Wells Fargo Center
400 capitol Mall, Suite 900
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-232
Dear Mr. Grant:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of William E.
Simon, Jr. regarding the financial disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act"),1 and incorporates the supplemental information you provided on Novemb er 16,
2001. You have asked the following specific questions:
QUESTIONS
1. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to complete Schedule A-2 of the
Statement of Economic Interests ("SEI") for Partnership IPP96, LP?
2. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule A-l his pro
rata share of Logistical Simon and each of its investments?
3. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule A-1 his pro
rata share of Geologistics Corporation and each of its investments?
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9 - 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
lgls.-


File No. A-01-232
Page No. 2
4. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule B his pro
rata share of any real property owned by Logistical Simon, LLC?
5. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule B his pro
rata share of any real property owned by Geologistics Corporation?
6. 
What are Mr. Simon's duties with respect to reporting on Schedule A-l
investments made and held by a limited partnership, of which Mr. Simon is a limited
partner owning less than a TOo/o interest, that has a long-standing and consistently applied
policy of refusing to disclose to its limited partners the identities and values of its
investments?
CONCLUSIONS
1. 
Yes. Mr. Simon's pro rata share of IPP96,LP exceeds l0%, which
requires that his interests therein be reported on Schedule A-2.
2. 
Because his pro rata share of Logistical Simon, LLC is 10o/o or greater,
Mr. Simon is required to report his interest on Schedule A-2, rather than on Schedule A-
1, and each of Logistical Simon's investments would also be reported on Schedule A-2.
3. 
No. Mr. Simon is not required to report his pro rata share of Geologistics
Corporation on Schedule A-1.
4. 
Because Mr. Simon's pro rata share of Logistical Simon exceeds l0%, his
interest in that business entity is reported on Schedule A-2. Therefore, Mr. Simon is
required to report the real property owned by Logistical Simon. However, the real
property is reported directly on Schedule A-2, rather than on Schedule B. Additional
pages may be attached as necessary.
5. 
No. Mr. Simon is not required to report any real property owned by
Geologistics Corporation.
6. 
Mr. Simon is only required to report his interest, which is less than IlYo,
in the limited partnership on Schedule A-1. There is no requirement that the identities or
values of the partnership's investments be reported. Mr. Simon's gross income received
from that partnership would be reported on Schedule C.
FACTS
Bill Simon is a candidate for the Republican nomination for the office of
Governor and intends to stand for nomination election at next March's statewide primary
He is currently preparing his Form 700 for filing in November.


File No. A-01,-232
Page No. 3
Mr. Simon owns various financial interests in business entities that in turn own
financial interests in other business entities that in turn own financial interests in still
other business entities, and so on.
Fact Pattern # 1:
Mr. Simon and his wife and dependent children (hereinafter "Mr. Simon") own a
17.17% interest in WESKIDS, LP, which in turn owns a 75.23% interest in IPP96, LP.
IPP96, LP, owns a93.27Yo interest in Logistical Simon, LLC, which in turn owns an
approximate 20oh interest in Geologistics Corporation, which in turn owns numerous
investments and real property. (We assume for these purposes that Mr. Simon's "pro rata
share" of these interests and assets exceeds the minimum necessary to trigger
reportability, i.e. $2,000, and that the entities are either located or doing business in
California.)
Visually, the financial relationships could be depicted as follows
Mr. Simon (17.17%) Other Owners (82.83%)
I
WESKIDS,LP (75.23yo)
Other Owners (24.77%)
I
IPP96, LP (93.27%) Other Owners (6.73%)
I
Logistical Simon, LLC (*20%) Other Owners (+80%)
I
Geologistics Corporation
I

Business Entityl
Assets
WESKIDS, LP
IPP96, LP
Logistical Simon, LLC
Geologistics Corporation

Corporation
Direct Ownership
by Mr. Simon

7
"Pro Rata Share" Pro Rata Share
of Mr. Simon Greater Than l0Yo
17.17% 
Yes
12.92% (75.23% of 17.17%) Yes
r2.0s% (93.27% of 12.92%) Yes
+2.4Yo (*20% of 12.05%) No
*2.4Yo (t20% of 12.05%) No


File No. A-01-232
Page No. 4
Fact Pattern # 2
Mr. Simon, his wife, and his dependent children (hereinafter "Mr. Simon")
together own a 50o/o interest in South Street Capitil,, LP, which in turn owns a 0.1565%
interest in Stone Bridge Partners Equity Fund III, LLP, which in turn owns numerous
investments and real property.
Visually, the relationships can be depicted as follows:
Mr. Simon (50%) 
Other Owners (50%)
I
South Street Capital,
LP (o.1s6s%)
Other Owners
(ee.843s%)
Pro Rata Share
Greater Than lOY"
I
Stone Bridge Partners Equity Fund III, LLP
I
Real Prop ertyllnvestments
Business Entity/
Assets
Direct Ownership
by Mr. Simon
6'Pro Rata Share"
of Mr. Simon
South Street Capital, LP
Stone Bridge Partners
Equrf Fund III, LP
Assets of Stone Bridge
Partners Equity Fund
50% 
Yes
0.0782s% (50% of 0.1565%) No
0.07825% (50% of 0.1565%) No
5j%o
0%
0%
Fact Pattern # 3
In addition, one of the partnerships in which Mr. Simon is a limited partner, with
a share of less than l}Yo, is a partnership whose sole purpose is to make investments in
other companies. The partnership has a long-standing and consistently applied policy of
refusing to disclose to its limited partners the identities and values of the investments it
holds. Therefore, except for a few investments that the partnership has been required to
disclose pursuant to the federal securities laws, Mr. Simon does not possess any
information about the identities and values of the partnership's investments, nor does
Mr. Simon have any legal right, under applicable statute or under the partnership
agreement, to compel the partnership to disclose such information.
ANALYSIS
Under the Act's conflict of interest provisions, public offrcials at all levels of state
and local government must file periodic statements disclosing certain personal financial
interests that may be affected during the exercise of their official duties. In addition,


File No. A-01-232
Page No. 5
Section 87201requires every candidate for an office specified in section 87200 other than
a justice of an appellate court or the Supreme Court to file, "no later than the final filing
date of a declaration of candidacy, a statement disclosing his or her investments, his or
her interests in real property, and any income received during the immediately preceding
12 months."
"Investment" is defined in section 82034 as follows:
"'Investment' means any financial interest in or security
issued by a business entity, including but not limited to
common stock, preferred stock, rights, warrants, options,
debt instruments and any partnership or other ownership
interest owned directly, indirectly or beneficially by the
public offrcial, or other filer, or his or her immediate
family, if the business entity or any parent, subsidiary or
otherwise related business entity has an interest in real
property in the jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do
business in the jurisdiction, or has done business within the
jurisdiction at any time during the two years prior to the
time any statement or other action is required under this
title. No asset shall be deemed an investment unless its fair
market value equals or exceeds two thousand dollars
($2,000). The term'investment' does not include a time or
demand deposit in a financial institution, shares in a credit
union, any insurance policy, interest in a diversified mutual
fund registered with the Securities and Exchange
Commission under the Investment Company Act of 1940 or
a common trust fund which is created pursuant to Section
1564 ofthe Financial Code, or any bond or other debt
instrument issued by any government or government
agency. Investments of an individual includes apro rata
share of investments of any business entity, mutual fund, or
trust in which the individual or immediate family owns,
directly, indirectly or beneficially, a lO-percent interest or
greater. The term 'parent, subsidiary or otherwise related
business entity' shall be specifically defined by regulations
of the Commission."
First Fact Pattern
Under the facts you have provided in the first scenario, Mr. Simon2 owns a
17.17% interest in WESKIDS, LP, which in turn owns a 75.23% interest in IPP96, LP,
giving Mr. Simon a I2.92Yo pro rata share of IPP96,LP. IPP96, LP owns a93.27Yo
2 Under the Act, Mr. Simon is required to report any investments owned by himself or members of
his "immediate family." (Section 82034.) An official's "immediate family' includes his or her spouse and
dependent children. (Section 82029.)


File No. A-01-232
Page No. 6
interest in Logistical Simon, LLC, making Mr. Simon's pro rata share of Logistical
Simon, LLC lZ.OsYo. Logistical Simon, LLC owns an approximately 20Yo interest in
Geologistics Corporation, which, in turn, owns real property and investments.
Mr. Simon's pro rata share of Geologistics Corporation and its assets is2.4Yo.
For purposes of reporting on a Statement of Economic Interest, sections 87201
and 82034 require Mr. Simon to report investments he holds in a business entity3 if the
value of the investment is $2,000 or more and if the business entity is doing business in
California. Sections 87206,87207 and 87209 pertain to the actual disclosure required on
the schedules attached to the SEI. An ownership interest in a partnership is an
"investment" for purposes of the Act. (Section 82034.) You have stated that Mr.
Simon's pro rata shares of the above referenced business entities exceed $2,000 and that
all of the business entities are doing business in California. Therefore, Mr. Simon's
reporting requirements are as follows:
Mr. Simon is required to report his ownership interest in WESKIDS, LP on
Schedule A-2 of his SEI. He must also report IPP96, LP on that Schedule A-2 as an
investment of WESKIDS, LP. IfWESKIDS, LP owns any real property, this would also
be reported on Schedule A-2. If Mr. Simon has received any income from WESKIDS,
LP, the gross income received would also be reported in Part 2 of Schedule A-2. "Gross
income" is the total amount of income received before deducting expenses, losses, or
taxes. (Wentland Advice Letter, No. I-98-050.) In addition, Mr. Simon is required to
report any single source of income to WESKIDS, LP if Mr. Simon's pro rata share of the
gross income to WESKIDS, LP from that source is $10,000 or more. Under the Act, an
official's income includes a community property interest in the income of his or her
spouse. (Section 82030(a).) Because Mr. Simon owns his interest in WESKIDS, LP
with his wife, for reporting purposes, his pro rata share of gross income from sources of
income to WESKIDS, LP is equal to the income from the percentage of the business that
Mr. Simon owns plus one-half of Mrs. Simon's income. (Kellett Advice Letter, No. A-
96-268; King AdviceLetter, No. I-99-145.) If this amount equals or exceeds $10,000,
then that source must be reported on Part 3 of Schedule A-2 of the SEI.
On a second Schedule A-2,becatse his pro rata share exceeds l)Yo, Mr. Simon
must report his interest in IPP96, LP, and any gross income received from IPP96, LP. On
that Schedule A-2, he must also show that IPP96, LP holds an interest in Logistical
Simon, LLC. If IPP96, LP owns any real property, that must also be reported on the
Schedule A-2. Once again, if there are any sources of income to IPP96, LP where
Mr. Simon's pro rata share of the gross income to IPP96, LP from that source is $10,000
or more, calculated in the same manner as explained pertaining to WESKIDS, LP, this
must be reported in Part 3 of Schedule A-2.
On a third Schedule A-2, because his pro rata share exceeds 10%, Mr. Simon
must report his ownership interest in Logistical Simon, LLC, and must also disclose that
3 "'Business entity' means any organization or enterprise operated for profrt, including but not
limited to a proprietorchip, partnership,ftrmu business trus! joint venture, syndicate, corporation or
association. " (Section 8200 5) [Emphasis added. ]


File No. A-01-232
Page No. 7
Logistical Simon, LLC holds an interest in Geologistics Corporation. If Logistical
Simon, LLC owns any real property, this must also be disclosed on this Schedule A-2.
Any gross income received from Logistical Simon,LLC, must also be reported inPart2
of the Schedule A-2. The real property of Geologistics Corporation need not be
disclosed. If there is any individual entity that is a source of income to Logistical Simon,
LLC where Mr. Simon's pro rata share of the gross income to Logistical Simon,LLC
from that source is $10,000 or more, calculated in the same manner as explained in
relation to WESKIDS, LP, this must be reported in Part 3 of Schedule A-2.4
Second Fact Pattern
Under the second scenario you have presented, Mr. Simon owns a 500/o interest in
South Street Capital, LP, which in turn owns a 0.1565% interest in Stone Bridge Partners
Equity Fund III, LLP, which in turn owns numerous investments and real property.
Mr. Simon is required to report his ownership interest in South Street Capital,LP,
on Schedule A-2, because that interest exceeds l0%. Also on Schedule A-2, Mr. Simon
would report the gross incomes received from South Street Capital, LP, and would report
that South Street Capital, LP holds an investment in Stone Bridge Partners Equity Fund
ilI, LP. The assets of Stone Bridge Partners Equrty Fund would not need to be disclosed.
If there is any individual entrty that is a source of income to South Street Capital, LP
where Mr. Simon's pro rata share of the gross income to South Street Capital, LP from
that source is $10,000 or more, calculated in the same manner as explained in relation to
WESKIDS, LP, this, also, must be reported in Part 3 of Schedule A-2.
Third Fact Pattern
With respect to your question regarding the limited partnership in which Mr.
Simon's interest is a less than l0o/o limited partnership interest, whose long-standing and
consistently applied policy is to refuse to disclose to its limited partners the identities and
values of the investments it holds, Mr. Simon's interest does not reach the threshold of
having to be reported on Schedule A-2, which would require disclosure of the
investments the partnership holds. Rather, Mr. Simon's interest in this partnership should
be reported on Schedule A-1, with any gross income from this partnership being reported
on Schedule C.
a For those items of gross income not already reported on Schedules A-1, A-2 or B, but
aggregatingto $500 or more received from any source located in or doingbusiness in his reporting
jurisdiction, Mr. Simon is required to report on Schedule C his gross income, which includes his
community property interest in his spouse's gross income.
s \rlr. Simon's gross income would include his entire gross income plus his community property
interest in Mrs. Simon's gloss income, i.e., one-half of Mrs. Simon's gross income.


File No. A-01-232
Page No. 8
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
6
By: Holly
Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

disclosed. If there is any individual entity that is a source of income to Logistical Simon,
LLC where Mr. Simon's pro rata share of the gross income to Logistical Simon,LLC
from that source is $10,000 or more, calculated in the same manner as explained in
relation to WESKIDS, LP, this must be reported in Part 3 of Schedule A-2.4
Second Fact Pattern
Under the second scenario you have presented, Mr. Simon owns a 500/o interest in
South Street Capital, LP, which in turn owns a 0.1565% interest in Stone Bridge Partners
Equity Fund III, LLP, which in turn owns numerous investments and real property.
Mr. Simon is required to report his ownership interest in South Street Capital,LP,
on Schedule A-2, because that interest exceeds l0%. Also on Schedule A-2, Mr. Simon
would report the gross incomes received from South Street Capital, LP, and would report
that South Street Capital, LP holds an investment in Stone Bridge Partners Equity Fund
ilI, LP. The assets of Stone Bridge Partners Equrty Fund would not need to be disclosed.
If there is any individual entrty that is a source of income to South Street Capital, LP
where Mr. Simon's pro rata share of the gross income to South Street Capital, LP from
that source is $10,000 or more, calculated in the same manner as explained in relation to
WESKIDS, LP, this, also, must be reported in Part 3 of Schedule A-2.
Third Fact Pattern
With respect to your question regarding the limited partnership in which Mr.
Simon's interest is a less than l0o/o limited partnership interest, whose long-standing and
consistently applied policy is to refuse to disclose to its limited partners the identities and
values of the investments it holds, Mr. Simon's interest does not reach the threshold of
having to be reported on Schedule A-2, which would require disclosure of the
investments the partnership holds. Rather, Mr. Simon's interest in this partnership should
be reported on Schedule A-1, with any gross income from this partnership being reported
on Schedule C.
a For those items of gross income not already reported on Schedules A-1, A-2 or B, but
aggregatingto $500 or more received from any source located in or doingbusiness in his reporting
jurisdiction, Mr. Simon is required to report on Schedule C his gross income, which includes his
community property interest in his spouse's gross income.
s \rlr. Simon's gross income would include his entire gross income plus his community property
interest in Mrs. Simon's gloss income, i.e., one-half of Mrs. Simon's gross income.

QUESTION: 1. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to complete Schedule A-2 of the
Statement of Economic Interests ("SEI") for Partnership IPP96, LP?
2. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule A-l his pro
rata share of Logistical Simon and each of its investments?
3. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule A-1 his pro
rata share of Geologistics Corporation and each of its investments?
lgls.-
4. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule B his pro
rata share of any real property owned by Logistical Simon, LLC?
5. 
Based on the relationships of WESKIDS, LP; IPP96, LP; and Logistical
Simon, LLC as presented herein, is Mr. Simon required to report on Schedule B his pro
rata share of any real property owned by Geologistics Corporation?
6. 
What are Mr. Simon's duties with respect to reporting on Schedule A-l
investments made and held by a limited partnership, of which Mr. Simon is a limited
partner owning less than a TOo/o interest, that has a long-standing and consistently applied
policy of refusing to disclose to its limited partners the identities and values of its
investments?

CONCLUSION: 1. 
Yes. Mr. Simon's pro rata share of IPP96,LP exceeds l0%, which
requires that his interests therein be reported on Schedule A-2.
2. 
Because his pro rata share of Logistical Simon, LLC is 10o/o or greater,
Mr. Simon is required to report his interest on Schedule A-2, rather than on Schedule A-
1, and each of Logistical Simon's investments would also be reported on Schedule A-2.
3. 
No. Mr. Simon is not required to report his pro rata share of Geologistics
Corporation on Schedule A-1.
4. 
Because Mr. Simon's pro rata share of Logistical Simon exceeds l0%, his
interest in that business entity is reported on Schedule A-2. Therefore, Mr. Simon is
required to report the real property owned by Logistical Simon. However, the real
property is reported directly on Schedule A-2, rather than on Schedule B. Additional
pages may be attached as necessary.
5. 
No. Mr. Simon is not required to report any real property owned by
Geologistics Corporation.
6. 
Mr. Simon is only required to report his interest, which is less than IlYo,
in the limited partnership on Schedule A-1. There is no requirement that the identities or
values of the partnership's investments be reported. Mr. Simon's gross income received
from that partnership would be reported on Schedule C.
